approvalRuleConfig.doc: Rule config — number of required approvals, eligible approvers, reapproval policy, self-approval toggle.

targetActionTypes.doc: Which action types the rule gates (e.g., deployment, environment_change).

scheduleCron.doc: Cron expression for recurring rotations. Mutually exclusive with `timestamp`.

timestamp.doc: RFC3339 timestamp for a single rotation. Mutually exclusive with `scheduleCron`.

revision.doc: Revision number the tag points to.

project.doc: Project the environment belongs to (defaults to "default").

yaml.doc:
The environment definition as YAML text. ESC documents carry
secrets; the property is marked secret so Pulumi state keeps
it encrypted.

enabled.doc: Whether the export is currently active. Set to false to pause without deleting.

organizationName.doc: Organization whose audit log is being exported.

s3Configuration.doc:
S3 destination settings — an object with `iamRoleArn`
(role Pulumi Cloud assumes to write), `s3BucketName`,
and optional `s3PathPrefix`.

enforceSAML.doc: When true, require SAML for all org members (no local-credential fallback).

metadataUrl.doc: SAML metadata URL (IdP publishes this). Mutually exclusive with `metadataXml`.

metadataXml.doc: SAML metadata document content (XML). Mutually exclusive with `metadataUrl`.

organizationName.doc: Organization the identity provider is configured for.

providerType.doc: Identity provider protocol. Only `saml` is supported today.

checks.message: exactly one of metadataUrl or metadataXml must be provided

accountName.doc: Account name. Must be unique within the organization.

environment.doc: ESC environment providing the provider credentials (format "<project>/<env>" or just "<env>" for default project).

organizationName.doc: Organization the insights account belongs to.

providerConfig.doc: Provider-specific configuration (region list, subscription IDs, GCP projects, etc.).

scanSchedule.doc: How often to scan this account automatically. Set to "none" to disable scheduled scans (manual invoke via `triggerScan` still works).

scheduledScanEnabled.doc: Whether scheduled scans are currently enabled for this account.

maxExpirationSeconds.doc: Maximum validity of access tokens exchanged via this issuer.

name.doc: A human-readable name for this issuer.

organization.doc: Organization the OIDC issuer belongs to.

policies.doc: Authorization policies controlling which workloads can exchange OIDC tokens for Pulumi tokens.

thumbprints.doc: TLS certificate thumbprints trusted for this issuer. Usually derived automatically.

githubTeamId.doc: Numeric GitHub team ID. Required for `github` teams.

members.doc: List of usernames in the team.

name.doc: Team name. Must be unique within the organization. Required for `pulumi` teams.

teamType.doc: Team type. "pulumi" for a Pulumi-managed team; "github" for a team synced from a GitHub organization.

destination.doc: Optional destination settings for rendered templates.

organizationName.doc: Organization the template source belongs to.

sourceName.doc: Human-readable name for this template source.

sourceURL.doc: URL of the template source (typically a git repository).

autoRemediate.doc: When true, automatically run an update to reconcile detected drift.

scheduleCron.doc: Cron expression for how often drift detection runs.

pulumiOperation.doc: Which Pulumi operation to run on schedule.

scheduleCron.doc: Cron expression for recurring schedules. Mutually exclusive with `timestamp`.

timestamp.doc: RFC3339 timestamp for a single-time run. Mutually exclusive with `scheduleCron`.

agentPoolId.doc: Agent pool ID this stack's deployments run on.

executorContext.doc: Executor context — agent pool, docker image, or custom runner.

github.doc: GitHub VCS settings — repository, branch/path filters, and push triggers.

operationContext.doc: Operation context — pre-run commands, environment variables, and OIDC settings.

sourceContext.doc: Source context — git coordinates or other VCS location.

vcs.doc: Generic VCS settings (used when `github` isn't applicable).

deleteAfterDestroy.doc: When true, delete the stack itself (not just resources) after the destroy succeeds.

timestamp.doc: RFC3339 timestamp after which the TTL destroy runs.

active.doc: Whether the webhook is currently firing.

displayName.doc: Human-readable display name for the webhook.

environmentName.doc: Environment the webhook belongs to (ESC-scoped webhooks only).

filters.doc: Event filter strings restricting which events trigger the webhook.

format.doc: Payload encoding — "raw" by default; other options shape the body for specific consumers.

name.doc: Webhook name, assigned by the server.

projectName.doc: Project the webhook belongs to (stack- or ESC-scoped webhooks only).

secret.doc: Shared secret used to sign webhook payloads (HMAC-SHA256).

stackName.doc: Stack the webhook belongs to (stack-scoped webhooks only).

checks.message: stackName and environmentName are mutually exclusive; omit both for an org-scoped webhook, set one for the matching scope

permission.doc: Permission level: 0 = no permission, 101 = read, 102 = edit, 103 = admin.

tags.doc: Tag name → tag value map managed by this resource.
